title: carya × collina
intro: Carya × collina, also known as the hickory persimmon, is a rare hybrid deciduous tree that belongs to the walnut family (Juglandaceae). The bark and roots of the Carya × collina plant are used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiviral properties. The health benefits of Carya × collina include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and boosting the immune system. The herbal preparations of Carya × collina include decoctions, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat various health conditions, including fever, respiratory issues, and skin problems.
